name: Natural inflation
summary: Inflation driven by a pseudo-Nambu-Goldstone boson (axion-like) field with a periodic potential V(phi) = Lambda^4 (1 - cos(phi/f)). The shift symmetry phi -> phi + const is broken only by the cosine term, naturally protecting the slow-roll potential against radiative corrections. Successful slow-roll requires f >~ M_Pl, which makes the model suspect from the weak-gravity-conjecture perspective.

addresses_puzzles.note: Predicts r ~ 0.05-0.13 for f ~ M_Pl - 5 M_Pl; current Planck + BICEP/Keck bounds r < 0.036 substantially exclude single-axion natural inflation. Aligned / N-flation extensions with f_eff > M_Pl still struggle with weak-gravity-conjecture pressure.

touches_benchmarks.note: Single-axion natural inflation with f ~ M_Pl predicts r ~ 0.07-0.13, ruled out by BICEP/Keck. Aligned-axion variants survive in narrow windows.
Predicts n_s ~ 0.95-0.97 for N=60 e-folds in the surviving parameter space.

new_symmetries: approximate shift symmetry phi -> phi + const
introduces.note: f and Lambda set the potential shape; protected by the shift symmetry.

history: Freese, Frieman, Olinto (1990), 'Natural inflation with pseudo Nambu-Goldstone bosons', Phys. Rev. Lett. 65, 3233. Aligned natural inflation (Kim-Nilles-Peloso 2004) and N-flation (Dimopoulos-Kachru-McGreevy-Wacker 2008) effectively raise the decay constant; BICEP/Keck has substantially constrained the framework, with weak-gravity-conjecture arguments adding theoretical pressure.

references.citation: Freese, Frieman, Olinto, 'Natural inflation with pseudo Nambu-Goldstone bosons', Phys. Rev. Lett. 65, 3233 (1990)
